results.notes: All checkable claims about the 2010 McGraw & Warren Psychological Science paper (authors, venue, year, core benign-violation two-condition claim, five-experiment finding that benign moral violations elicit amusement alongside disgust, three benign-making levers: alternative norm/weak commitment/psychological distance) are confirmed. The 2012 and 2014 follow-up papers and the Hurricane Sandy study are about other papers not in this transcription and were excluded per instructions.

Author (Marvin Minsky), title, year (1980, MIT AI Memo No. 603), and characterization ('casting humor as debugging of faulty reasoning') all confirmed against the abstract's framing of humor as recognizing/suppressing 'bugs -- ineffective or destructive thought processes.' The 'direct ancestor of Inside Jokes' claim is cross-paper lineage, not checkable here.

discrepancies.guide_says: Dataset: Thousands of cartoons with finalist captions
discrepancies.paper_says: The corpus is 704 cartoons (hundreds), with 2.7K high-quality captions (thousands). 'there are 2.7K high quality captions across 704 contests'; 'For each of 704 cartoons'

results.notes: Authors, three tasks, and all quantitative claims confirmed: best from-pixels model 62% (62.3) on 5-way matching vs 94% (94.0) human; GPT-4 5-shot 84.5% matching in From Description (human scene descriptions); human explanations preferred over GPT-4's in 67.7% (>2/3). Only the 'thousands of cartoons' phrasing is off — it is thousands of captions but 704 cartoons.

Authors (Jentzsch & Kersting), method (prompt-based generation/explanation/detection), 1,008 jokes, 'over 90% collapsed onto the same 25 jokes' (917 of 1008), and 'explained real jokes well but fabricated plausible explanations for non-jokes' all confirmed. Guide's 'GPT-3.5 era' vs paper's 'GPT3-based model' is a harmless characterization of the ChatGPT-early-2023 model, not flagged.

Authors (all 8 names/spellings), year (2025, arXiv:2507.21476), method (LLM-judge grading explanations against discrete verifiable elements), dataset (~300 cartoon-caption pairs, NYCC top-3 finalists + Cartoonstock), and all findings (STEM transfer, STEM-only models do well, mixed test-time gains, no LLM >60% on HumorBench-hard; o3 best at 87.5% main / 59.85% hard) confirmed.

Authors, PunEval 2,589, JOKER 632/632, 7 LLMs + RoBERTa, temp 0, reformulation method, human eval, and 'regressive sycophancy' all confirmed. 'EMNLP 2025' is a venue/status claim the arXiv preprint neither confirms nor contradicts; not flagged per rules.

Authors, 600 jokes (150 each of 4 types), human reference explanations, 4,800 explanations (600x8), zero-shot, topical hardest, no model reliably explains all four, DeepSeek-R1 math/code reasoning not transferring, LLM judge + automatic metrics + topical case study all confirmed.

Authors (Xu, Yuan, Chen, Yang), 3 tasks with new ICL-suited metrics, biased prompts, SemEval-2017 Task 7 + ExPun, 8 LLMs, lower TNR than TPR, 'lazy pun generation', strong-models-respectable all confirmed. Transcription page-1 header renders the title pun as 'reward' (OCR artifact); body confirms 'reword'.

Authors (all 8), augments SemEval-2017 pun corpus with keywords + NL explanations + funniness ratings, 'first humor dataset with such fine-grained annotations for puns', supports explanation and generation all confirmed.

Authors, ACL 2025 (explicitly stated on page 1), novelty/anti-contamination curation, fixed structural schema, three tasks + error analysis, and 'most LLMs struggle to generalize, consistently underperforming the human baseline' all confirmed.

unverifiable: venue 'CHum 2025': no venue line in transcription; not contradicted
results.notes: Author (Ashwin Baluja), text + TTS spoken form method, CoT-as-explanation, and multimodal-beats-text-only across all datasets confirmed.

unverifiable: venue 'Findings of ACL 2024': not self-stated; not contradicted
results.notes: Author list, ChinesePun 2.1k (1,049 homophonic + 1,057 homographic), multi-stage curriculum preference learning with improved DPO, Chinese + English evaluation, 'significantly outperforms all baselines' confirmed.

unverifiable: venue 'CVPR 2024': arXiv v3 states no CVPR line; not contradicted
results.notes: Authors, 'CoT not conducive to creative problem-solving', instruction-tuning + explorative self-refinement, Oogiri-GO 130k+ multimodal/multilingual, transfer to other creativity tasks confirmed.

'Published as a conference paper at ICLR 2025' confirmed verbatim; multi-hop reasoning, RL with GPT-4o reasoning extraction, external knowledge injection, improves judgment and generation, positioning against CLoT all confirmed.

Kim & Chilton (Columbia), pipeline (observe/diverge/generate 30/Gen-Z agent ranks top 5), 20 images x 15 captions, 'significantly funnier than GPT-4o', 'on par with top IG captions, p=0.053' all confirmed.

discrepancies.guide_says: JAPE ... used WordNet plus pronunciation dictionaries
discrepancies.paper_says: The 1994 JAPE paper describes no WordNet and no pronunciation dictionary; its homonym source is 'a separate homonym base derived from a list ... of homophones in American English, shortened considerably.' (WordNet/pronunciation dictionaries belong to the successor STANDUP, not this paper.)

The 'pathetic' verdict came from the adult judging panel: JAPE-1's 188 jokes were scored by '14 judges', producing 'jokes, but pathetic ones'. Schoolchildren are mentioned only as a hypothetical future evaluation group.

results.notes: Binsted & Ritchie, exact title, AAAI attribution confirmed; the 'pathetic' quote exists but is misattributed, and WordNet is a STANDUP-era tool.
IUI 2025, three-condition method, 335 human + 307 collaborative images (150/condition sampled), 'more ideas / less perceived effort' confirmed verbatim.

Author list/order, 365 contests (530-895), >2.2M captions, >250M ratings (284,183,913), funny/somewhat/unfunny, ~8 years, NEXT bandit platform, SFT hurts, RLHF/DPO underperform, GPT-4 & Claude trail top humans all confirmed. Paper self-labels 'Preprint'; NeurIPS claim not contradicted.
Small-scale alignment, 20 caption pairs per contest from 379 contests (#510-#889), 279 train / 100 test, expert-level ranking (67% -> 82.4%) confirmed.

First author Vural, IRS three stages, supervise-the-trace framing, CPT -> SFT -> alignment pipeline, NYCC matching/ranking, theory groundings (incongruity-resolution, script opposition, frame-shifting) confirmed.

Gorenz & Schwarz, PLoS ONE 19(7):e0305364 2024, two blind studies, three comedy tasks, Onion comparison, ~200 raters (N=200, N=217), 'funnier than most laypeople', 'no less funny than professional comedy writers' confirmed.

discrepancies.guide_says: multi-task across crosstalk generation, recognition, type/level classification, and punchline detection
discrepancies.paper_says: CFunModel's tasks are Crosstalk Response Selection, Humor Recognition, Joke Generation/Continuation, Humor Explanation, and Crosstalk Generation (Table 1; Sec 4). 'Humor type classification, humor level classification, and punchline detection' appear only in Related Work as tasks proposed by Chen et al. (2024a) — not tasks of this paper's model.
CFunSet comprises 'more than 160,000 entries'; the 20,000 figure is only the Tieba-JokeBar subset ('over 20,000 jokes collected from Tieba—JokeBar').

results.notes: Findings claim ('surpasses general LLMs on Chinese humor recognition') and year/preprint status confirmed; only the method characterization has issues.
